codeReview.summary: The feature adds a deterministic monthly walk-forward runner, persistence outputs, and focused tests, but the committed implementation still misses two blocking requirements from the feature contract: it never recomputes feature data per walk-forward step, and the default CLI path backtests an uncalibrated identity model instead of the current calibrated pipeline.

issues.description: `run_walk_forward_backtest()` resolves the training dataset once (`_resolve_training_data()` at lines 171-178), and `_resolve_training_data()` builds/caches a single parquet once for the whole requested span (`build_training_dataset()` at lines 669-674). Each monthly window then just slices that already-materialized dataframe in `_evaluate_window()`. The feature description explicitly required 'Anti-leakage: recompute features at each step'; this implementation never regenerates per-window features, so it does not actually satisfy that requirement and cannot prove the walk-forward inputs were rebuilt from only information available at each monthly step.

The backtest defaults to `calibration_method = "identity"` (lines 47 and 317), while the current calibration pipeline defaults to `platt` in `src/model/calibration.py:43,215,416`. `.factory/services.yaml:27` invokes `python -m src.backtest.run` without overriding that flag, so the reviewed CLI/backtest path measures an uncalibrated stack rather than the project’s current calibrated inference path. That breaks backtest-to-production parity and makes the reported Brier/ROI metrics non-representative of the model configuration the rest of the ML pipeline now uses.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared state does not clearly record the canonical calibration method that downstream features should use. Mission/library docs still describe the stack as isotonic-calibrated, while the live calibration code defaults to Platt scaling; this ambiguity likely made it easy for the walk-forward feature to choose an `identity` default and drift away from the actual inference path.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission docs still describe an isotonic stack at `C:\Users\bills\.factory\missions\83c0c194-72d1-4821-8b08-68a3497c3590\mission.md:7,33-34` and `C:\Users\bills\Documents\Personal Projects\MLBPrediction2026\.factory\library\architecture.md:19,53-55`, but the live code defaults to Platt at `src/model/calibration.py:43,215,416`. The reviewed feature then defaulted to identity at `src/backtest/walk_forward.py:47,317`, and the manifest backtest entrypoint at `.factory/services.yaml:27` provides no calibration override.

summary: Review failed. The implementation is organized and reproducible, but it does not fulfill the explicit per-step feature recomputation requirement and the shipped CLI path backtests an identity-calibrated model instead of the project’s current calibrated stack, so the feature is not yet an accurate walk-forward validation of the intended pipeline.
